title: Enforce convention articulation on planning artifacts via requires-edge invariants; error in .context, warning in the shipped catalog
context: Conventions govern decisions/features/tasks but are only passively composed into context, so an LLM can skip them. The validator's requires-edge engine (config.invariants[], the vocabulary-free loop at context-sdk.ts:2055-2078) already enforces edge-presence — decision-cites-forcing-artifact is the living template — so articulation can be enforced as config with no validator code.
decision: Register two relation_types: item_governed_by_convention (decisions/features/tasks -> conventions) and item_acknowledges_missing_convention (decisions/features/tasks -> framework-gaps). Add three requires-edge invariants, one per source block (decisions, features, tasks), each requiring at least one edge of either relation_type. When no convention governs an artifact, file a 'no convention governs X' framework-gap and acknowledge it via the second relation_type, surfacing missing governance as tracked debt rather than a silent opt-out. Severity is error in .context (hard reject, installed only after the existing artifacts are backfilled) and warning in the shipped samples catalog so a fresh substrate — which has no conventions yet — is advised, not blocked.
consequences: The .context error invariant is installed only AFTER backfilling every existing decision/feature/task, else the substrate is invalid.
No pi-context code change — pure config (relation_types + invariants) in .context now, and in samples/conception.json for the catalog.
A new planning artifact must articulate a convention or file a missing-convention gap to validate.
Missing-convention gaps drive authoring the missing conventions over time.
